comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report, 3:45 PM

Today's conditions were grouse (see daily photo).

The last 48 hours brought a dramatic swing in temperatures, forcing patrol to close a significant amount of terrain, but our Mountain Ops team is the best in the business at thaw/freeze recovery (it's why we're known as the king of spring). Now that it's cold enough for snowcats to safely get on the hill, our grooming team can work their magic.

Tonight, it's all hands on deck. They're starting with the trails off SuperQuad and Bucksaw Express, then heading east. They're taking their time to deliver the best possible surface, so expect a delayed opening for Whiffletree lift. Next, they'll move to the trails off King Pine, where they plan to spend the entire day. King Pine and its associated trails will be closed tomorrow to allow groomers to make the surface as soft and consistent as possible for the weekend.

The lift schedule for tomorrow will be Snubber, SuperQuad, Skyline, Bucksaw Express, Skidway, and DRC-B at 8:30 AM followed by Whiffletree as soon as groomers are clear of its trails. King Pine and Timberline will not spin.

Our parks crew is also ready to rip. They'll be working all through tonight and tomorrow and aim to have all parks, besides Jill Poke, reopened for Saturday. They may even have a few features on Skybound for tomorrow.

Patrol will assess conditions for uphill travel tomorrow morning, check Uphill Status below prior to starting a climb.

To top it all off (literally), we have snow on the way Friday night. Forecasts are calling for 2-5 inches so far, but check back for updates tomorrow. The numbers are trending up, which is always a good sign.

While Mountain Ops is focused on delivering the best possible experience on the hill, our events team has a stacked weekend lineup, including Vintage Ski Day, the 75th Anniversary Gala, and more. Check the events calendar for the full schedule.

Enjoy the ride,
Alex

comment.name: Uphill
comment.text: Uphill travel is currently closed due to conditions.
